prompt: The merge sort implementation produces unsorted output. What is the bug?
choices: The base case l >= r should be l > r
mergeSort(arr, mid, r) includes mid in the right half; it should be mergeSort(arr, mid+1, r) to avoid processing the midpoint twice
merge() should be called before the recursive calls
mid should be computed as (l + r + 1) / 2 to handle odd-length arrays
explanation: When the right recursive call is mergeSort(arr, mid, r), the element at index mid is included in both the left sort [l..mid] and the right sort [mid..r]. This causes incorrect partitioning. Fix: mergeSort(arr, mid+1, r) so the right sub-array starts at mid+1.
